proof:
-- Unfold the definition of is_egocentric
      -- We need to show I ◁ I = I
      -- From I.call, we have ∀ x : Bird, I ◁ x = x
      -- Applying this to x = I, we get I ◁ I = I
      exact_mod_cast (I.call I)
failed_attempts:
unfold IsEgocentric
      have h1 : I ◁ I = I := by 
        specialize I_call I
        simpa using I_call
      exact h1

-- The definition of "I is egocentric" is I ◁ I = I
      -- Let's apply I_call to x = I
      have h := I_call I
      -- h is now I ◁ I = I, which is exactly the goal
      exact h

-- The goal is I is egocentric, which is I ◁ I = I
      -- This is exactly what I_call states when applied to I
      apply I_call

-- need to show I is fond of I
      unfold IsEgocentric
      -- need to show I is fond of I
      unfold IsFondOf
      -- need to show I ◁ I = I
      -- apply I_call with I
      exact I_call I
simp [show I ◁ I = I by apply I_call]

unfold IsEgocentric
      have h1 : I ◁ I = I := by
        apply I_call
      simpa using h1

-- By definition of I, we have I ◁ I = I
      exact I_call I

unfold is_egocentric
      -- We need to show I ◁ I = I
      -- From I_call, we have ∀ x, I ◁ x = x
      -- Applying this to I, we get I ◁ I = I
      exact I_call I
exact I_call I
simp [I_call]

-- We need to show I is egocentric, which is I ◁ I = I
      -- From the definition of I, we have that I is a Bird, and I_call : ∀ x : Bird, (I ◁ x) = x
      -- Applying I_call to x = I, we get (I ◁ I) = I, which is exactly the definition of I being egocentric
      exact I_call I
exact I_call I
simp [I_call]

-- We need to show I ◁ I = I
      -- From the definition of I, we know that I ◁ x = x for any x : Bird
      -- Applying this to x = I, we get I ◁ I = I, which is exactly what we need.
      exact I_call I

unfold is_egocentric
      have h1 : I ◁ I = I := by 
        apply I_call
      exact h1
simp [I_call]

unfold is_egocentric
      exact I_call I
exact I_call I

-- The definition of I is egocentric is I ◁ I = I
      -- From I_call, we have ∀ x : Bird, I ◁ x = x
      -- Applying this to x = I, we get I ◁ I = I
      -- So we can use I_call with I as the argument
      have h1 : I ◁ I = I := I_call I
      -- The goal is I is egocentric, which expands to I ◁ I = I
      -- So we can use h1 to prove the goal
      exact h1

have h1 : I ◁ I = I := I_call I
  exact h1

unfold IsEgocentric
      have h1 : I ◁ I = I := I.call I
      exact h1

unfold I.is_egocentric
      exact I.call I

unfold is_egocentric
      have h := I.call I
      exact h
simp [I_call]

-- We need to show I ◁ I = I
      -- We have the axiom I_call: ∀ x : Bird, (I ◁ x) = x
      -- Applying this to x = I, we get (I ◁ I) = I, which is exactly what we need
      apply I_call

-- We need to show I is egocentric, which is I ◁ I = I
      -- We have the hypothesis I_call : ∀ x : Bird, (I ◁ x) = x
      -- Applying I_call to x = I, we get (I ◁ I) = I
      exact_mod_cast (I_call I)

unfold is_egocentric
      apply I_call I
exact I_call I

unfold IsEgocentric
      apply I_call

have h : I ◁ I = I := by
        apply I_call
      exact h
successful_attempts:
-- Unfold the definition of is_egocentric
      -- We need to show I ◁ I = I
      -- From I.call, we have ∀ x : Bird, I ◁ x = x
      -- Applying this to x = I, we get I ◁ I = I
      exact_mod_cast (I.call I)

-- To prove I is egocentric, we need to show I ◁ I = I
      -- From I.call, applying x = I, we get (I ◁ I) = I
      simpa using I.call I
